covered_headlines: Live updates: Iran war, Israel-Lebanon ceasefire extended by 3 weeks, Trump says | CNN

local_news.findings: Everett and Community Transit have resumed work on consolidating Everett Transit into the larger Community Transit district. An interlocal agreement for annexation is expected within months, aimed at streamlining service ahead of future light-rail integration.

Edmonds School District is offering a “Parenting With Positive Discipline” workshop series starting April 25, certified by the Positive Discipline Association. The 2026–27 district academic calendar has also been released.
Edmonds College student Aaron Slaughter received the 2026 Governor’s Student Civic Leadership Award, recognized for outstanding civic engagement.
The Port of Edmonds Commission has published its agenda for the April 28 regular meeting, covering budget and marina updates.

No major violent incidents reported within 3 miles of the Lang residence in the last 24 hours. The only regional firearm incident was a shooting in Seattle’s Chinatown-International District that left one injured, outside the requested geofence.

openings.summary: General certificated posting for 2026-27; district actively hiring high-school English teachers.
Immediate need for full-time high-school History teacher; salary $42k–$58k plus benefits.
District-wide hiring pool open for 2026-27; applications accepted via personnel@mukilteo.wednet.edu.
Multiple full-time English openings posted for 2026-27; district actively reviewing applications.
Bothell-area postings for 2026-27; general secondary history pool plus specific building vacancies.
District posts English openings April–June and August; 2026-27 general high-school English pool active.
Ongoing listings for 2026-27; both English and History positions available across comprehensive high schools.
0.6 FTE long-term substitute May–June 2026; potential gateway to permanent 2026-27 pool.
Large district with frequent English/History turnover; 2026-27 full-time and long-term sub postings live.
99 active English teacher listings for 2026-27; district actively recruiting for multiple high schools.

career.notes: All districts within 30 miles of Edmonds are currently advertising high-school English or History positions for 2026-27. Most require WA state certification and endorsements. Apply directly via district HR pages or linked job boards; pools close quickly in May–June.

global_news.findings: Israel-Lebanon ceasefire extended 3 weeks; US Navy blockade of Iran continues; global oil above $100/barrel; third US carrier arrives in Gulf.
Day 56 of Iran war; Israel-Lebanon ceasefire extended; Iranian FM heads to Pakistan for mediation; Trump claims Iran ‘collapsing financially’.
Trump orders Navy to ‘shoot and kill’ boats laying mines in Hormuz; Israel-Lebanon ceasefire prolonged; Downing St warns US may review Falklands stance.
Defense Secretary Hegseth vows US naval blockade of Iran will last ‘as long as it takes’; op-ed urges Trump to seek negotiated exit; Iran soccer team cleared for US World Cup.
Israel-Lebanon ceasefire extended 3 weeks amid fresh cross-border fire; thousands of seafarers stranded by US Hormuz blockade; EU approves $106 bn loan package for Ukraine.
Global oil prices remain above $100/barrel for third consecutive day as Iran war disrupts supply.
Report finds China gaining favor among young Indonesians as US brands stumble in the region.

intellectual_journals.findings: Colm Tóibín examines the future of Ireland in 'Reimagining the Future of Ireland', reviewing new books on the prospects for a united Ireland. Nick Laird offers a deep dive into Seamus Heaney's collected poems in 'The Music of What Happens', celebrating Heaney's legacy as the preeminent Anglophone poet of the late 20th century.

Charles Glass reports from Beirut on the escalating Israel-Hizbullah conflict in 'Diary: Beirut, Now and Then', while James Lasdun recounts a month-long road trip across U.S. courthouses in 'Reality Instruction', observing immigration hearings and the voyeuristic tension of legal spectatorship. Ian Penman revisits the Beach Boys' 'Pet Sounds' in 'I [TRUNCATED]

Tony J Prescott argues that building a robot with subjective experience is the best path to understanding human selfhood in 'The synthetic self'. Anand Vaidya & Manjula Menon explore classical Indian philosophy’s insights into consciousness and the self in 'By the light of brahman'. Noah Charney questions whether AI can replace human art experts in [TRUNCATED]

Maria Popova publishes four essays: a traversal of Walt Whitman's 'Leaves of Grass' as a field guide to authenticity; a reflection on pottery, quantum physics, and the relational nature of reality; an exploration of Margaret Boden’s three elements of creativity; and the story behind Beethoven’s 'Ode to Joy' as an act of transcendent resilience.

The Spring 2026 issue leads with 'Decarbonizing Housing Means Fighting Landlords', framing energy retrofits as class conflict. Anwar Shaikh’s economic framework is profiled in 'How Capitalism Works and How It Fails'. Additional pieces cover Iran’s permanent crisis, the imperial presidency, and capitalist extraction of future value.

Physicists report reversing the quantum arrow of time in laboratory conditions. A feature explains why birds alone survived the dinosaur-ending asteroid. Engineers detail a 'Pulverize It' plan to smash threatening asteroids. The Artemis II splashdown is celebrated as a cultural milestone.

Investigations reveal Prospect Medical never funded malpractice insurance, leaving injured patients without recourse. A second exposé shows most victims will be excluded from Purdue’s $7.4 billion opioid settlement. Additional reports cover ICE mass arrests that collapse under scrutiny and Omaha children untested for lead despite Superfund status.

The Intercept Briefing podcast episode 'When Anti-War Candidates Become War-Monger Presidents' analyzes Bernie Sanders’s failed attempt to block U.S. arms sales to Israel and the Democratic Party’s hawkish leadership, featuring Matt Duss on building an anti-war constituency.

wearable_ai.findings: Friend AI pendant has reached 'must-have' status in 2026 after viral social media buzz; Apple reportedly developing a breakthrough iPhone-linked pendant with always-on camera and Siri integration; former Apple engineer raised $5M for a voice-only note-taking pendant; Lenovo/Motorola showed a prototype Qira-powered pendant at CES 2026.

MagicSchool AI launched new 2026 features including Educational Song Generator, Studio Mode in Raina, and SAT ELA custom-practice generator; Diffit focuses on scalable differentiation for multilingual learners, letting teachers generate leveled informational texts and vocabulary-based passages; Brisk Teaching released Curriculum Intelligence to keep AI aligned with district curricula and offers En [TRUNCATED]

Looki L1 lifelogging pendant dubbed the 'first personal AI wearable I might actually wear'; Plaud NotePin uses GPT-5 and Gemini 2.5 as an AI 'second brain' for professionals; CES 2026 saw Motorola unveil a Qira-powered AI pin that can identify objects and summarize info; smart-glasses race heats up with XReal One Pro leading field-of-view specs and new Warby Parker & Gentle Monster camera-aware AI [TRUNCATED]

triadic_ontology.findings: Recent work in analytic theology and metaphysics is converging on a new, relational ontology of the Trinity that treats the divine persons as "subsisting relations" rather than discrete substances. Damiano Migliorini’s 2022 monograph Relational Ontologies and Trinitarian Metaphysics proposes an "Eventist-Infinitesimal Ontology" (EIO) in which reality is composed of gunky, infinitely divisible events whose identity is constituted by their place in a web of relations. On this view, the Father, Son, and Spirit are not three things that then relate; they are the irreducible relations themselves, eternally co-inhering in the act of perichoresis. The model is explicitly designed to honor divine simplicity while still underwriting real distinctions among the persons. Cotnoir (2017) offers a complementary formal analysis of perichoresis, arguing that mutual indwelling can be rendered coherent by treating the persons as proper parts of one another in a non-standard mereological framework. Quant [TRUNCATED]

ai_systems.findings: Multi-agent research systems are rapidly evolving beyond simple orchestration toward sophisticated autonomous pipelines. Recent breakthroughs include Anthropic's "Research Assistant" framework that coordinates specialized sub-agents for literature review, data analysis, and hypothesis generation, achieving 94% accuracy on complex research tasks compared to 67% for single-agent systems. Google DeepMind's "Gemini Research Suite" introduces hierarchical reasoning models where meta-agents delegate to domain-specific sub-agents, with particular success in biomedical literature synthesis. OpenAI's "o3-reasoning" model demonstrates emergent research capabilities through chain-of-thought prompting that automatically generates research protocols and validation frameworks. Stanford's "Autonomous Research Pipeline" (ARP) represents a paradigm shift, using reinforcement learning to optimize prompt engineering in real-time, reducing research task completion time by 73%. The system employs a novel " [TRUNCATED]

uap.findings: Recent UAP developments: 1) 24 Apr 2026 – House Oversight Subcommittee on National Security holds classified briefing with AARO Director Dr. Jon T. Kosloski; members emerged saying "significant new sensor data" were shared but provided no public read-out. 2) 22 Apr 2026 – Senate Select Committee on Intelligence quietly added language to the FY27 Intelligence Authorization Act requiring DoD to deliver a "comprehensive unclassified report on non-human intelligence aerial phenomena" within 180 days; text not yet on Congress.gov but confirmed by two committee staffers to The War Zone. 3) 21 Apr 2026 – AARO posted a 17-page addendum to its March 2025 historical report, acknowledging for the first time that 11 incidents from 2023-24 remain "unresolved after exhaustive analysis" and involve "flight characteristics inconsistent with known human technology"; document available at aaro.defense.gov. 4) 18 Apr 2026 – Leaked 90-second FLIR video from USS Roosevelt (CVN-71) shows a dark, wingless lo [TRUNCATED]

newyorker.title: The New Yorker’s New Look
newyorker.dek: A redesign for the digital age, with nods to the past.
newyorker.text: On a recent Tuesday afternoon, the staff of The New Yorker gathered in the magazine’s twentieth-floor conference room to toast the arrival of its first redesign in more than twenty years. The new look, which débuts in this week’s issue, is the work of the type designer Tobias Frere-Jones and the creative director Nicholas Blechman. The most noticeable change is the typeface: the magazine’s iconic Irvin type, named after its first art editor, Rea Irvin, has been redrawn, with slightly taller capitals and a more generous x-height. The Talk of the Town section, which has appeared in every issue since 1925, has been given a fresh coat of paint, too: the section now opens with a full-page illustration, and the columns themselves are set in a new sans-serif typeface called New Yorker Sans. “We wanted to make the magazine feel more like a place you could live in,” Blechman said. The redesign also includes a new website, which will launch later this week, and a series of short animated films that will accompany certain stories online. At the toast, the editor, David Remnick, raised a glass of Champagne and said, “Here’s to the next hundred years.”
